demographics.living_situation: lives at home with both parents, who are his full-time carers

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: (Call made by the parents) He won't stop seizing, he has had three massive fits back-to-back, and he sounds like he is drowning in mucus.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Our son has been fitting for about 45 minutes, one after another, and he hasn't woken up in between. We gave his emergency medicine but he keeps seizing and now his breathing has gone all wet and gurgly.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 45 minutes ago the first fit started; it lasted around 4 minutes
symptom_narrative.character: big whole-body stiffening and jerking fits, one after another, and he has not woken up or been himself in between them; he is still doing little twitches in his arms and legs
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst he has ever had — we are terrified he is going to die
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: three fits back-to-back over about 45 minutes with no proper recovery in between
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing — we gave his rescue medicine into his cheek about half an hour ago and he still kept fitting
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it just keeps happening on its own
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: he was sick (vomited) during the second fit and straight after that his breathing turned loud, wet and gurgly; his lips have gone a bit blue and he feels hot
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he has seizures fairly often because of his brain condition, but never a run like this that would not stop
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: he is completely unresponsive right now and not waking up at all

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: alobar holoprosencephaly (a brain that did not form normally) — diagnosed as a baby
past_medical_history.how_managed: under a paediatric neurology and disability team; on daily anti-seizure medicine

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: severe global developmental delay and profound intellectual disability — he is non-verbal, cannot walk, and is fully dependent on us for everything
hormone problems from his brain condition (something with his pituitary/hypothalamus gland)
cleft lip and palate (a gap in his lip and the roof of his mouth)
long-standing severe feeding and swallowing difficulties

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: emergency rescue medicine given into the cheek for long fits (midazolam)

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: cared for full-time at home by both parents; we have a suction machine at home for his mucus but the pot is full and it isn't working right now

paediatric_context.developmental_concerns: known severe global developmental delay and profound intellectual disability from a congenital brain malformation; non-verbal and non-ambulant, so he cannot describe or localise symptoms himself

objective_data_offered.value: he is completely unresponsive and not waking up, with small ongoing twitching movements of his arms and legs
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: parent description over the phone; not a clinician neurological assessment
lighting and phone camera make colour hard to judge reliably

objective_data_offered.value: loud, wet, gurgling breathing that started right after he vomited during a fit

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: hand-felt temperature, no thermometer reading given

objective_data_offered.value: home suction machine is present but the collection pot is full so it is not working
